metadata.notes: National average baseline ~$550-750/window installed (survey of 16 brands). All prices reflect fully installed per-window cost (product + labor) unless noted.

northeast.notes: Higher labor, prevailing wage in some markets, historic home premiums

west.notes: CA labor + Title 24 energy compliance upgrades

florida_coastal.notes: Impact/hurricane code drives premiums in coastal FL counties; inland FL ~0.95

installMethodMultiplier.notes: Full-frame premium is +40% to +100% over pocket/insert. Average premium $600-$1,000/window over insert. Required when existing frame is rotted, out of square, or enlarging opening.

Renewal by Andersen.notes: Fibrex composite only; factory-direct full-frame; 2-3x dealer Andersen

ProVia.notes: Top vinyl; heavy frame, triple-pane option

value_vinyl_per_window.label: Value Vinyl (Window World / Atrium / Alside Sheffield)

premium_wood_clad_per_window.label: Premium Wood-Clad (Andersen A-Series / Pella Reserve / Marvin Signature / RbA)

commonUpsells.notes: Per window. Worth it in Northern/North-Central climate zones; marginal elsewhere.
Per window. Required for ENERGY STAR compliance. Significant energy savings.
Per window. Standard on ENERGY STAR units; usually included.
Per window. Needed if existing trim is rotted or when aesthetically upgrading.
Per window. Needed on full-frame replacement or damaged trim.
Per window. Cosmetic only; between-glass cheaper than simulated divided lite.
Per window. Required by code in FL coastal counties and other hurricane zones.
Per window. Required if existing frame is rotted or out of square.

scopeCheckItems.label: U-factor and SHGC (NFRC label data) listed
Permits pulled and paid by contractor
Haul-away and disposal of old windows included
Warranty terms stated in writing (transferability, fees)
Order date, lead time, and target install date listed
Labor rate or install labor stated

Northern.label: Northern (heating-dominated)
Northern.priority: Low U-factor; SHGC unrestricted (higher SHGC helps via passive solar)

energyStar.notes: SHGC unrestricted; alternative path U<=0.26 + SHGC>=0.32 or U<=0.28 + SHGC>=0.42

mostEfficient.notes: Qualifies for 25C federal tax credit; LSG (VT/SHGC) >= 1.10
Qualifies for 25C federal tax credit; LSG >= 1.10
Qualifies for 25C federal tax credit; LSG >= 1.10

iraCredit.notes: 30% of product cost only (not labor), capped at $600/year for windows/skylights. Requires ENERGY STAR Most Efficient certification and manufacturer Qualified Manufacturer PIN on Form 5695.

utilityRebates.utility: Mass Save (Eversource, National Grid, Unitil, Cape Light)
utilityRebates.eligibility: Triple-pane ENERGY STAR Northern Most Efficient; replacing single-pane; whole-home assessment required
utilityRebates.claimMethod: Post-install via Mass Save portal; contractor can file

utilityRebates.eligibility: Income-qualified (EmPower+) or any homeowner (Comfort Home); BPI-certified contractor
Tiered by U-factor; U<=0.22 for max; triple-pane bonus; registered contractor preferred
ENERGY STAR; BPI contractor (Home Comfort program)
Electric heat home; ENERGY STAR North-Central/Northern
Electric heat primary; ENERGY STAR Northern U<=0.22
ENERGY STAR Northern; Trade Ally contractor
Gas/electric customer; ENERGY STAR; replacing single-pane

redFlagPatterns.name: today_only_urgency
redFlagPatterns.regex: \b(today only|tonight only|expires? (today|tonight|at midnight)|sign (today|tonight|now)|valid (today|for 24 ?hours)|manager[' ]?s? special|truck ?load (pricing|special)|one[- ]time offer|limited time)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Signature urgency pressure. Legitimate window quotes are valid 7-30 days; manufacturer pricing does not change hourly. Also bypasses the 3-day federal right-of-rescission mental checkpoint.
redFlagPatterns.action: Refuse to sign in the same visit. Get quote validity in writing (7-30 days minimum).

redFlagPatterns.name: lifetime_warranty_void_traps
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Lifetime warranty with fine-print maintenance voids (quarterly track cleaning, approved cleaners only, mail-in inspection cards). Most homeowners void these terms within 12 months.
redFlagPatterns.action: Request full warranty PDF by email. Confirm in writing that normal household cleaning does not void coverage.

redFlagPatterns.name: one_time_transfer_warranty
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Warranty transfers only once (often with $50-$250 fee). Homeowners resell within 7 years on average, so this is effectively a 7-year warranty, not lifetime.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ask explicitly how many times the warranty transfers and whether there's a fee. Get the answer in writing.

redFlagPatterns.name: trim_capping_billed_separately
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Capping, trim, drywall repair, or paint billed as 'as needed' or separately. Can add $75-$300 per opening discovered after walls are open when you have no leverage to walk away.
redFlagPatterns.action: Demand all-inclusive quote. Ask: 'Does this price include exterior capping, interior trim, drywall repair, and paint?'

redFlagPatterns.name: deferred_interest_balloon
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Deferred interest financing trap. At month 25, full retroactive interest at 27.99% APR hits, or a balloon payment 40% of balance is due. FTC and CFPB have issued alerts.
redFlagPatterns.action: Get the Truth-in-Lending disclosure. Confirm whether it's 'deferred interest' vs '0% simple.' Assume balloon until proven otherwise.

redFlagPatterns.name: free_install_pricing_inversion
redFlagPatterns.explanation: 'Buy 4 get installation free' marks up the per-window price 30-60% to absorb the 'free' install. Obscures true unit cost and makes comparison impossible.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ask for line-item breakdown: window unit cost, installation, disposal, permit.

redFlagPatterns.name: subcontractor_handoff
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Install is performed by 1099 subcontracted crew with no brand accountability. Warranty disputes become finger-pointing between brand and sub. Quality varies wildly.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ask in writing: 'Will the company on this contract perform installation with W-2 employees, or is it subcontracted?' Get installer license number.

redFlagPatterns.name: missing_disposal
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No mention of haul-away or disposal. Crew may charge $25-$75/window on install day or leave old units stacked in driveway. Pre-1978 windows have EPA RRP disposal requirements.
redFlagPatterns.action: Confirm 'haul-away and EPA-compliant disposal of existing windows' is a line item in the written scope.

redFlagPatterns.name: homeowner_responsible_permit
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Quote pushes permit responsibility to homeowner. Permits for whole-home window replacement run $150-$800. Unpermitted work can fail home inspection at resale.
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must say 'contractor pulls and pays all required permits.'

redFlagPatterns.name: vague_scope_language
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Verbal promises without written scope detail. 'Per standard installation' is unenforceable. If it's not written, it does not exist.
redFlagPatterns.action: Every promise goes on the contract or walk away.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_license_number
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No state contractor license number on quote. AARP's #1 elder fraud category for home improvement. Deposits taken, work never starts.
redFlagPatterns.action: Verify state contractor license, BBB rating, and Google Business Profile before any deposit.

redFlagPatterns.name: vague_product_spec
redFlagPatterns.explanation: 'Premium vinyl' with no manufacturer, series, or model number. Prevents NFRC lookup and allows bait-and-switch to a cheaper SKU at install.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require manufacturer name, series, model number, and glass package on every line item.

redFlagPatterns.name: missing_nfrc_ratings
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No U-factor, SHGC, or NFRC label data. You cannot verify ENERGY STAR eligibility or 25C federal tax credit qualification without these numbers.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require NFRC label data on every line: U-factor, SHGC, VT, air leakage.

redFlagPatterns.name: install_type_not_specified
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Quote doesn't say pocket/insert vs full-frame. Price difference is $200-$600 per opening. You may be paying full-frame prices for a pocket install.
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must specify one of: insert/pocket, full-frame, or new construction.

redFlagPatterns.name: manager_approval_theatre
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Salesperson theatrically 'calls the manager' for a 40-50% discount off an inflated sticker. The original price was never real.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ignore the sticker. Judge only the final number against 2-3 competing quotes.

redFlagPatterns.name: excessive_deposit
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Deposit over 33%. Industry standard is 10-33% at signing, balance on completion. Large deposits are a leading indicator of abandonment fraud. Several states cap home improvement deposits (MD 33%, ME 33%).
redFlagPatterns.action: Cap deposit at one-third. Remainder on substantial completion.

redFlagPatterns.name: vague_timeline
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No specific install date. Window orders take 4-12 weeks from manufacturer. Deposits sit with contractor indefinitely without accountability.
redFlagPatterns.action: Contract must state order date, manufacturer lead time, and target install date with a not-to-exceed clause.

redFlagPatterns.name: out_of_state_po_box
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Address is a PO box or out of state with no local showroom/license/phone. Warranty service becomes impossible. State AGs cite out-of-state storm-chaser operations.
redFlagPatterns.action: Verify physical local address, in-state contractor license, and local reviews.

recommendedScopeItems: Brand, series, and model number for every window
NFRC label data: U-factor, SHGC, VT, air leakage
Install type specified: pocket/insert or full-frame
Line-item breakdown: unit cost, labor, disposal, permit, trim
Contractor pulls and pays all required permits
Haul-away and EPA-compliant disposal of existing windows
Interior trim and drywall repair included
Warranty terms in writing: transfer count, fees, voids
ENERGY STAR certification and climate-zone compliance
Manufacturer PIN/QM code provided for 25C tax credit
Order date, manufacturer lead time, target install date
Deposit capped at 33% with balance on substantial completion
Installer is W-2 employee (or named subcontractor with license)
State contractor license number on the contract
questionsToAsk: Is this quote valid for at least 30 days in writing?
What is the exact brand, series, and model number of each window?
What are the U-factor, SHGC, and VT on the NFRC label?
Is this a pocket/insert or full-frame replacement?
Does the price include exterior capping, interior trim, drywall repair, and paint?
Who pulls and pays for the permit — you or me?
Is haul-away and disposal of my old windows included?
Will your W-2 employees install, or is it subcontracted? What's the installer's license number?
How many times does the warranty transfer, and is there a fee?
Does normal household cleaning void the warranty?
What is the deposit percentage, and when is the balance due?
What's the order date, manufacturer lead time, and target install date?
Will you provide the manufacturer Qualified Manufacturer PIN so I can claim the 25C tax credit?
Does this window meet ENERGY STAR Most Efficient for my climate zone?
If I finance, is it deferred interest or 0% simple? May I see the Truth-in-Lending disclosure?
